scenario: Hard lockup and kernel panic due to excessive console printing by DellEMC PowerFlex (scini) module
symptoms: Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP
Hard lockup detected on CPU 29 on process rcu_sched
Soft lockups detected on CPUs 21 and 27, followed by rcu stalls on CPUs 20 and 7

workflow.thought: Check system information and panic message
workflow.observation: Panic string 'Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP'

workflow.thought: Examine backtrace of rcu_sched process on CPU 29
Compare hrtimer_interrupts values to confirm watchdog detection
Check hrtimer_interrupts value for same CPU
Resolve symbol address to identify spinlock structure
Examine rcu_node.lock structure to check lock state
workflow.observation: Lock shows pending=1, locked=0 in raw_lock

workflow.thought: Analyze lock value bitfields for detailed state
workflow.observation: Binary shows bits set at positions 20, 19, 8

workflow.thought: Search kernel log for soft lockup messages
workflow.observation: Soft lockups on CPU#21 and CPU#27

workflow.thought: Search for rcu stall messages in kernel log
workflow.action: log | grep -A3 'rcu_sched detected stalls' | grep -v Disconnected | grep -v PowerFlex

workflow.thought: Count PowerFlex error messages in kernel log
Get detailed module information for scini

root_cause_analysis: The cause of the hang/panic state of the system is excessive printing of errors on the console by DellEMC PowerFlex(scini).
Different types of errors reported but most of the time it was:PowerFlex netSock_GetMyAddress:1776 :Error: Failed to retrieve socket local address for socket 00000000559ecfe0 with Errno 16.
